text.title: Vision Accessibility — Alt Text & Scene Description Core Rules

rule.statement: Alt text MUST be ≤125 characters; MUST NOT start with 'image of' / 'picture of' / 'photo of'.
rule.rationale: Screen readers announce 'image' before the alt; the prefix double-reads.

rule.statement: Any text visible in the image MUST be included verbatim in alt or long_description.
rule.rationale: Visually impaired users must receive the same information as sighted users.

rule.statement: The surrounding page context (heading, section purpose, URL slug) MUST be passed to the VLM prompt.
rule.rationale: Context-free alt describes what is in the image but not why it is on the page.

rule.statement: Decorative images MUST get alt="" — never a generated description.
rule.rationale: Generated descriptions for decoration add noise to screen readers.

rule.statement: Chart alt text MUST state chart type AND the key data insight (trend, comparison, extreme).
rule.rationale: Screen reader users cannot derive insights from a visual; the alt must encode the conclusion.

rule.statement: Numerical values extracted from charts MUST be cross-checked against source data before being used as authoritative alt.
rule.rationale: VLMs hallucinate specific numbers on charts.
